consent.title: This site stores preferences locally
consent.description: We use your browser's local storage to save favorites, currency preference and theme. No data is sent to any server.

favorites.title: Your Favorites
favorites.locked_description: To save favorites, you need to accept data storage in the consent banner at the bottom of the page.
favorites.empty_description: Go to the dashboard and click the star on any cryptocurrency to save it here.

about.title: About CryptoInsight
about.subtitle: CryptoInsight is an open-source educational platform for exploring cryptocurrency market data. It is built to help beginners understand the crypto ecosystem through real data, clean visualizations and straightforward information — without noise, hype or financial advice.
is_items: A dashboard for visualizing real-time market data for the top 10 cryptocurrencies by market cap
An educational tool for people who want to understand crypto without being overwhelmed
A portfolio project demonstrating modern frontend engineering practices
Powered entirely by public APIs — no server, no backend, no accounts required
is_not_items: Not a crypto exchange, wallet or trading platform
Not a source of financial or investment advice
Not affiliated with any cryptocurrency project
about.data_source_text: All market data is fetched live from the CoinGecko public API. CoinGecko is one of the largest independent cryptocurrency data aggregators, tracking over 10,000 coins across 700+ exchanges. Prices and market data are updated in real time on their end.
about.data_source_note: Note: The free tier of the CoinGecko API has rate limits. If data fails to load, wait a moment and retry.
about.privacy_text: CryptoInsight stores only three things in your browser's local storage: your favorite coins, your selected currency, and your theme preference. No personal data is collected, no analytics are run, and nothing is sent to any server. You can revoke storage consent at any time from the dashboard.
about.author_text: Designed and built by Alden Merlin. This project is open source and intended as a reference for clean frontend architecture using modern React tooling.

landing.hero_description: CryptoInsight is a free educational platform that helps beginners explore the world of cryptocurrencies — with real market data, plain language explanations and no financial hype.
landing.what_is_p1: A cryptocurrency is a form of digital money that exists only online. Unlike the dollar or the euro, no government or bank controls it. Instead, it runs on a technology called blockchain — a public, tamper-proof record book maintained by thousands of computers around the world simultaneously.
landing.what_is_p2: Bitcoin was the first cryptocurrency, created in 2009. Since then, thousands of others have emerged — each with different goals, technologies and communities. Some aim to be digital cash, others power smart contracts or decentralized applications.
landing.concepts_subtitle: The essential ideas you need to follow the crypto world.

concepts.blockchain_desc: A chain of data blocks where every transaction ever made is permanently recorded. No single entity controls it — the record is shared and verified by thousands of computers globally.
concepts.bitcoin_desc: The original cryptocurrency, created in 2009 by the pseudonymous Satoshi Nakamoto. Often called 'digital gold', Bitcoin has a fixed supply of 21 million coins.
concepts.ethereum_desc: A programmable blockchain that allows developers to build decentralized applications (dApps). Its native currency is Ether. Think of it as a global computer, not just money.
concepts.wallet_desc: A digital tool that stores your private keys — the passwords that prove ownership of your crypto. Wallets don't actually store coins, just the access credentials.
concepts.marketcap_desc: Current price × circulating supply. It measures the total market value of a cryptocurrency, similar to how we measure the size of a company on the stock market.
concepts.liquidity_desc: Volume is how much of a coin was traded in 24 hours. High volume = high liquidity = easier to buy and sell without moving the price drastically.
concepts.mining_desc: The process by which new Bitcoin transactions are verified and added to the blockchain. Miners compete to solve a cryptographic puzzle — the winner adds the next block and earns a reward.
concepts.decentralization_desc: No single authority controls a decentralized network. This removes the need to trust a bank or government — the rules are enforced by code and consensus, not institutions.

landing.market_data_intro: When you open the dashboard, you'll see live data for the top 10 cryptocurrencies. Here's what each number means:

market_data_terms.price_def: The current value of 1 unit of that coin in your selected currency (USD, EUR or BRL).
market_data_terms.change_def: How much the price moved in the last 24 hours, shown as a percentage. Green = went up, Red = went down.
market_data_terms.mcap_def: Price × total circulating supply. The bigger the market cap, the more established the coin is considered.
market_data_terms.volume_def: Total value traded in the last day. High volume means more people are actively buying and selling.
market_data_terms.chart_def: A visual of the price over the past week. Helps spot short-term trends — is it recovering? Crashing? Stable?
market_data_terms.ath_def: All-Time High and All-Time Low — the highest and lowest prices ever recorded for that coin.

landing.disclaimer_text: CryptoInsight is an educational tool only. The data shown is for informational purposes. Cryptocurrency markets are highly volatile — prices can drop dramatically in hours. Never invest more than you can afford to lose, and always do your own research before making any financial decision.

faq.a1: It depends on how you define 'real'. Crypto is a legitimate asset that can be bought, sold and used for transactions. However, unlike government-issued currencies, it has no legal tender status in most countries and its value is determined entirely by supply and demand.
faq.a2: Through a centralized exchange like Coinbase, Binance or Kraken. You create an account, verify your identity, deposit money and then purchase the coin you want. CryptoInsight is not an exchange and cannot help you buy crypto — we only show data.
faq.q3: Why do prices change so much?
faq.a3: Crypto markets are global, operate 24/7 and have relatively small market caps compared to traditional assets. This makes them highly sensitive to news, regulation announcements, large trades and market sentiment.
faq.q4: What's the difference between Bitcoin and Ethereum?
faq.a4: Bitcoin was designed primarily as digital money and a store of value. Ethereum is a programmable platform — it can run smart contracts and decentralized applications. Both are leading cryptocurrencies but serve different purposes.
faq.a5: The underlying blockchain technology is cryptographically secure. The risks come from human factors: losing your private keys, using fraudulent exchanges, falling for scams or making uninformed investment decisions. Knowledge and caution are your best tools.
faq.a6: CryptoInsight is a free educational tool built to help beginners understand crypto through real data. It is not a wallet, exchange or financial advisor. All data comes from the CoinGecko public API.

landing.cta_subtitle: Live data for the top 10 cryptocurrencies by market cap.
